title: UX Scan
findings: Found 2 concrete UX issues: install-v2 can duplicate semantic-search guidance/prompts, and `fabric info scope` without an argument reaches runtime scope resolution instead of usage validation.

issues_found.title: install-v2 runs semantic-search guidance twice after successful pipeline install
issues_found.description: The new install pipeline includes GuidanceStage, whose documented responsibility includes handling semantic search and whose execute path prompts/enables it. After pipeline success, runInitCommand preserves the old post-install semantic-search block and calls the same prompt/enabling logic again. In an interactive install this can ask the same question twice; with --enable-embed it can print duplicate enablement guidance in one command run.
issues_found.fix_direction: Move semantic-search onboarding to a single owner. Prefer keeping it inside GuidanceStage and deleting the legacy post-install block in install-v2, or make GuidanceStage purely presentational and keep all mutation prompts after the pipeline.

issues_found.title: `fabric info scope` without scope argument falls through to runtime crash
issues_found.description: The `scope` positional is optional, but the scope branch calls `runScopeExplain(args.scope!)`. The scope validator uses RegExp.test(scope), and the shared regex accepts the coerced string `undefined`; the built dist then reaches resolver code and throws a raw TypeError stack trace instead of showing usage or an actionable missing-argument error.
issues_found.fix_direction: Require the scope positional when subcommand is `scope`, or explicitly guard in resolveMode/runScopeExplain and render `fabric info scope <scope>` usage with a nonzero exit before calling scopeExplain.
